author: izdeby
text: Why is THPVariable_invert implemented on the python level and not c++

author: izdeby
text:
It looks like THPVariable\_invert should be implemented in C++ to avoid duplicate work from a JIT team.

For more details, please, see [PR](https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/21113) comments.

author: izdeby
text:
izdeby mentioned this on Jun 26, 2019
[BC-BREAKING] Changed tensor comparison return type from uint8 to bool #21113

author: colesbury
text:
To clarify, it's [written in C++](https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/blob/5f84f372a6fcd265bdd00f1f4481a47815ee6fc3/tools/autograd/templates/python_variable_methods.cpp#L285-L299) but it's defined in python\_variable\_methods.cpp not in ATen.

For the "why" part, it was because of the funky name in Python (`__invert__`) and because it was implemented as a simple one-liner: `1 - self`.

The name turns out not to matter. We have other native\_functions defined with double underscore prefixes, so that should be OK. It should totally be fine to move it to ATen.
